description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical base constructed from gray masonry blocks, giving it a robust and classic appearance. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof, covered in weathered shingles that show signs of age and wear, providing shelter from the elements. Two vertical wooden posts support the roof and a horizontal wooden beam, which holds a hand-crank mechanism attached to a rope for drawing water. The crank is made of metal and wood, and is mounted on one side of the well. The interior of the well is open and dark, indicating depth and the presence of water below. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the structure is likely intended for public or communal use in an urban or park setting.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a weathered wooden roof and a hand-crank mechanism for drawing water. The structure is sturdy, functional, and lacks decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden posts, and the shingled roof with visible weathering.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hand-crank mechanism and the supporting wooden frame under the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the stone cylinder and the underside of the roof, with no crank visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle and the full height of the protective stone wall.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A hand-crank mechanism with a rope is present, mounted on a horizontal wooden beam, made of wood and metal.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A cylindrical stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well, serving as a protective barrier.
